items.headline: Swiatek withdraws from Madrid Open after falling ill in match
items.description:
MADRID (AP) — Iga Swiatek withdrew from the Madrid Open due to an unspecified illness while playing her round-of-32 match against American Ann Li on Saturday.
Li got the walkover win when the fourth-ranked Swiatek withdrew with Li winning 7-6 (4), 2-6, 3-0.

items.headline: Bayern storm back to beat Mainz ahead of PSG clash
items.description: Bundesliga champions Bayern Munich stormed back from three goals down to win 4-3 at Mainz on Saturday, ahead of their Champions League semi-final...

items.headline: Blue Jays put Lukes on IL with hamstring strain, promote Piñango and will start Yesavage on Tuesday
items.description:
TORONTO (AP) — The Toronto Blue Jays put outfielder Nathan Lukes on the 10-day injured list Saturday because of a strained left hamstring and selected outfielder Yohendrick Piñango from Triple-A Buffalo.
Also Saturday, the Blue Jays said right-hander Trey Yesavage will make his season debut by starting against Boston on Tuesday. Yesavage began the season on the injured list as he recovered from a shoulder impingement.

items.headline: Brad Keselowski needs a little bit of luck to keep up with the Toyotas at Talladega
items.description:
TALLADEGA, Ala. (AP) — Brad Keselowski felt the utmost confidence entering Talladega Superspeedway this weekend — even while knowing his car doesn’t have the speed to keep up with the Cup Series leaders.
Keselowski has six wins at the 2.66-mile tri-oval, tying him with Dale Earnhardt Jr. and Jeff Gordon for second in NASCAR history. Only Dale Earnhardt Sr., who won a record 10 times at the Alabama track, has more victories there.
